phases.name: Positioning
phases.description: Define project scope and separation from bridge

phases.name: Extraction
phases.description: Resilient data extraction from unstable inputs

tasks.label: Partial read support
tasks.note: _read_drawers_resilient returns partial results; tested by test_good_rows_survive_bad_rows and test_partial_*

tasks.label: Invalid JSON tolerance
tasks.note: Extraction is SQLite-typed (no JSON parsing). Document/metadata strings are returned as-is. Tested by test_document_*json* and test_metadata_json_like_*.

tasks.label: Corrupted SQLite handling
tasks.note: PRAGMA integrity_check failure and missing required tables raise ExtractionError (CRITICAL). Mid-scan page error caught by while/next loop. Tested.

tasks.label: Record-level isolation
tasks.note: Each row processed independently; failure appended to failed_rows without affecting others. Tested by all _excluded tests.

tasks.label: No global crash
tasks.note: Pre-flight critical failures raise ExtractionError (not bare exceptions). Per-row failures isolated. Mid-scan DatabaseError caught and returns partial results. Tested by test_mid_scan_corruption_returns_partial.

tasks.label: Structured anomalies output
tasks.note: ctx.add_anomaly() called for every failure with type/severity/stage/message/context. FailedRow.to_dict() tested. Tested by test_anomaly_emitted_* suite.

tasks.label: Define anomaly enum types
tasks.note: AnomalyType (str, Enum) defined in core/context.py as a closed registry of every anomaly type the system can emit. Free-form type strings are rejected by add_anomaly via _coerce_type.

tasks.label: Define severity levels
tasks.note: Severity (str, Enum) with LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL. Invalid severities rejected by _coerce_severity. SEVERITIES tuple kept for stable ordering. Severity.HIGH == 'high' preserved for backward-compat string equality.

tasks.label: Define location schema
tasks.note: AnomalyLocation dataclass with required non-empty stage and optional source/identifier/record_pk/path/extra. Anomaly.stage delegates to location.stage.

tasks.label: Attach evidence model
tasks.note: AnomalyEvidence dataclass (kind, detail, data). Anomaly.__post_init__ enforces at least one evidence entry. All extraction and pipeline call sites migrated to attach structured evidence.

tasks.label: Remove unstructured logs
tasks.note: ctx.anomalies is the single channel for inconsistencies. No print/logging calls in extraction or pipeline. Detection still has its own structured Evidence/Contradiction model (predates M3 and remains structurally represented). Legacy add_anomaly(stage=..., context=...) shape still accepted but produces a structurally-valid Anomaly with synthesized AnomalyLocation + AnomalyEvidence(kind='legacy_context').

phases.name: Reporting
phases.description: Expose full system state and anomalies

tasks.label: Global stats
tasks.note: extraction_stats (total/parsed/failed/rate) + stages section (executed|aborted|skipped|not_run per stage) in build_report().

tasks.label: Anomaly aggregation
tasks.note: anomaly_summary extended with by_stage, top_severity (highest observed severity). by_type already existed. Derived from final anomaly_dicts list so consistency-invariant meta-anomaly is included.

tasks.label: Confidence summary
tasks.note: confidence_summary section: detection.band from DetectionResult.confidence_band, extraction.band from parse_rate thresholds, overall_band = weakest_band(detection, extraction). UNKNOWN when any signal is absent. Rule is explicit and documented.

tasks.label: Human-readable summary
tasks.note: reporting/text_renderer.py: pure render_text(report) function. CLI delegates entirely; no rendering logic in cli/.

tasks.label: Structured JSON output
tasks.note: schema_version=3. REPORT_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS tuple pins the contract. All sections are JSON-safe (tested by test_report_is_json_safe_strict without default=).

tasks.label: Structural validation
tasks.note: validation/structural.py: 3 checks (extraction_arithmetic, drawer_shapes, detection_evidence_nonempty). Severity cap HIGH. Verifies upstream object shapes without interpreting contents.

tasks.label: Internal consistency checks
tasks.note: validation/consistency.py: 4 checks (unique_drawer_ids, id_not_in_both_parsed_and_failed, failed_row_has_anomaly which externally verifies the M3 guarantee, stage_result_coherence).

tasks.label: Heuristic plausibility
tasks.note: validation/heuristics.py: 3 checks with module-level threshold constants (PARSE_RATE_PLAUSIBILITY_FLOOR=0.50, DOMINANT_FAILURE_TYPE_THRESHOLD=0.80). Severity capped at MEDIUM by design — heuristics never produce HIGH/CRITICAL.

tasks.label: Confidence-based output
tasks.note: ValidationResult.confidence_band ∈ {HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, UNKNOWN} via weakest-outcome rule. Integrated into report confidence_summary; overall_band = weakest_band(detection, extraction, validation).

tasks.label: Avoid 'correctness' claims
tasks.note: Trichotomous CheckStatus (passed/failed/inconclusive) — no boolean returns. checks_not_performed always populated (target_record_count_parity, target_id_set_parity remain skipped while reconstruction is a stub). Forbidden-vocabulary regex test enforces no 'correct/verified/guaranteed/safe/valid' in JSON output. validate() never raises MigratorError.

tasks.label: Input handling
tasks.note: click.Path(exists=True, dir_okay=True) on source arg; report subcommand accepts existing file. Nonexistent path rejected by Click before pipeline entry.

tasks.label: Output display
tasks.note: --json-output global flag: JSON to stdout only. Text via render_text(). --quiet suppresses all output while preserving exit code. _decide_exit_code() is the single authority on exit codes; exit 8 guards silent CRITICAL anomalies.

tasks.label: Execution modes
tasks.note: Three subcommands: analyze (ANALYZE_PIPELINE: detect+extract), inspect (FULL_PIPELINE: +transform/reconstruct stubs+validate), report (re-render saved JSON, no pipeline). PIPELINES registry in core/pipeline.py. No migrate/reconstruct commands.

tasks.label: Exit-code policy (_decide_exit_code)
tasks.note: Pinned codes: 0=ok, 2=detect, 3=extract, 4=transform, 5=reconstruct, 6=report, 7=validate, 8=success-but-critical-anomaly, 10=unexpected. Pure function; unit-tested in test_cli.py::TestDecideExitCode (11 cases).

phases.name: Adversarial Testing
phases.description: Break the system intentionally. Strategy fixed in tests/M7_ADVERSARIAL_DESIGN.md. No new stages, exit codes, severities, or features may be added by M7. Defects discovered are fixed in the owning production module, not absorbed in test code.

tasks.label: Corrupted JSON / blob cases (extraction-row level)
tasks.asserts: per-row anomaly with evidence.kind naming the bad column; row in failed_rows, not in parsed; exit 0 or 8; never crash.
tasks.note: 7 corpus entries; per-row contracts pinned for blank_embedding_id / control_chars_in_id / document_missing / document_null_string_value. JSON-agnostic cases (unparseable_metadata_string_value, document_multiple, metadata_all_null) are skipped here and covered by 10.6 invariants.

tasks.label: Broken SQLite cases (pre-flight + mid-scan)
tasks.asserts: detection or extraction rejection -> exit 2 or 3; CRITICAL anomaly attributed to the failing stage; no Python traceback escapes to stderr.
tasks.note: Detection's structural pre-scan catches sqlite_missing / zeroed_header / required_table_missing / multiple_collections / unexpected_collection_name BEFORE extraction; this is correct early-rejection behavior. Truncated SQLite remains outcome-dependent.

tasks.label: Mixed-format / contradictory-signal inputs
tasks.asserts: failure.stage='detect' on exit 2; detection records contradictions OR an inconsistency-kind evidence record OR drops band below HIGH; no silent classification.

tasks.label: Inconsistent data (duplicates / cross-set membership / collection mismatch)
tasks.asserts: validation summary_counts.failed + inconclusive > 0 on broken inspect runs; validation never silently claims all-passed; band != HIGH; an extract-stage anomaly explains the downgrade.

tasks.label: Extreme edge cases (empty, 0-byte, manifest-only, empty-palace inspect)
tasks.asserts: every edge case reaches a modelled exit code (0/2/3); non-zero exits emit at least one anomaly and populate report.failure with a non-empty stage; never exit 10; never raw traceback on stderr without --debug.

tasks.label: Cross-cutting invariants over the full adversarial corpus (the actual exit gate)
tasks.asserts: nine invariants hold for every corpus entry: no exit 10, no traceback w/o --debug, no silent CRITICAL, schema_version==REPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION, JSON-safe without default=, anomaly well-formedness, forbidden-vocabulary regex, stage attribution on raised errors, stdout/stderr discipline.
tasks.note: M7 surfaced one production defect: detection evidence string contained the forbidden word 'valid' on manifest JSON parse failure; fixed in src/mempalace_migrator/detection/format_detector.py. All 280 invariant assertions green across 28 corpus entries.

phases.name: Final Hardening
phases.description: Lock the runtime envelope (logging, latency, memory, repeatability) of the M1–M7 system as a recorded baseline so future drift is detected, not discovered. M8 adds NO new pipeline stages, NO new exit codes, NO new severities, NO new AnomalyType values, NO new CLI flags, NO production print/logging. Strategy fixed in tests/M8_HARDENING_DESIGN.md.
phases.implementation_status: done — tests/hardening/ implemented, baselines committed, 718 tests green

tasks.label: Logging discipline (structural ban via AST walk)
tasks.asserts: no print / logging / sys.std*.write call site under src/mempalace_migrator/; click.echo call sites confined to a documented allow-list in cli/main.py; --quiet stdout empty; --json-output stdout is exactly one JSON document.
tasks.note: Production code is currently free of these call sites (verified during M8 design pass). M8.1 turns the current good state into a pinned structural contract via an ast walk — NOT a regex — so aliased imports (from logging import getLogger as gl) are also caught.

tasks.label: Performance baseline (drift detector, not benchmark)
tasks.asserts: for every entry in baselines/runtime_envelope.json: median wall-clock over 5 subprocess runs <= recorded_p50 * (1 + tolerance.wall_clock_pct/100). Default tolerance: 50%.
tasks.note: Subprocess only — never CliRunner. Absolute wall-clock numbers are forbidden; only delta-vs-baseline is asserted. No flake-retry. Re-baseline only via tests/hardening/rebaseline.py in a reviewed PR.

tasks.label: Memory envelope (peak RSS via getrusage)
tasks.asserts: for every entry in baselines/runtime_envelope.json: observed_peak_rss <= recorded_peak_rss * (1 + tolerance.rss_bytes_pct/100). Default tolerance: 25%.
tasks.note: POSIX-only via resource.getrusage(RUSAGE_CHILDREN). Non-POSIX skip is asserted (no silent skips). tracemalloc inside the test process is forbidden — the CLI runs in a subprocess.

tasks.label: Stability invariants (the actual M8 exit gate)
tasks.asserts: nine invariants over the baseline corpus: report-signature stability, schema stability, exit-code stability, wall-clock envelope, RSS envelope, logging discipline (re-asserted), stdout/stderr discipline (re-asserted), all M7 invariants still hold on the baseline subset, determinism (two consecutive runs produce identical signature modulo run_id/started_at).
tasks.note: Imports M7 invariants from a refactored tests/adversarial/_invariants.py module — test-only refactor, no M7 assertion changes. Drift fails the suite; there is no --update-baseline flag.

phases.name: Transformation Stage
phases.description: Implement step_transform: pure, in-memory normalisation of ExtractionResult.drawers into a TransformedBundle ready for ChromaDB 1.x ingestion. No I/O, no writes, no chromadb import. Source material: _normalize_metadata, _validate_drawer_record, _analyze_drawers in mempalace-mcp-bridge:scripts/palace_reconstruction_prototype.py.
phases.implementation_status: done — all tasks complete, 90 new tests green, 672 total tests green, baselines regenerated
non_goals: No serialisation to disk (the prototype's drawers.jsonl bundle format is out of scope for the in-process pipeline)
No new exit codes (4 is already reserved for transform failure by M6.4)
No embedding re-computation
No new CLI flags

tasks.label: TransformedBundle dataclass
tasks.asserts: frozen dataclass with: collection_name (str), collection_metadata (dict[str, str|int|float|bool]), drawers (tuple[TransformedDrawer, ...]), summary (TransformedSummary). TransformedDrawer holds id (str), document (str), metadata (dict). TransformedSummary holds drawer_count, sample_ids, metadata_keys, wing_room_counts, length_profile (mirrors prototype _analyze_drawers output but typed).

tasks.label: Metadata normalisation (port _normalize_metadata)
tasks.asserts: normalize_metadata(value) -> tuple[dict, list[str]]. Accepts only str/int/float/bool values. Non-string keys, list/dict/None values rejected with issue messages. Returns (normalised_dict, issues). Pure function. Unit-tested for every rejected type.

tasks.label: Drawer integrity analysis (port _analyze_drawers + _validate_drawer_record)
tasks.asserts: analyze_drawers(drawers) -> DrawerAnalysis with id_integrity (duplicate_ids, blank_id_records), content_integrity (non_string_document_ids, empty_document_ids, length_profile), metadata_integrity (structural_issue_ids). Each issue is a structured record, not a string.

tasks.label: transform(ctx) entry point
tasks.asserts: transform(ctx) -> TransformedBundle. Reads ctx.extracted_data.drawers. Drops drawers with blank ids, non-string documents, unparseable metadata — each drop emits exactly one anomaly with stage='transform' and a new AnomalyType (TRANSFORM_DRAWER_DROPPED, TRANSFORM_METADATA_COERCED). Duplicates are dropped (matching prototype) with anomaly TRANSFORM_DUPLICATE_ID_DROPPED. Stores result on ctx.transformed_data.

tasks.label: Pipeline wiring
tasks.asserts: step_transform calls transformation.transform(ctx); removes the NOT_IMPLEMENTED anomaly emission. step_transform raises TransformError (new MigratorError subclass) on unrecoverable failure (e.g. extracted_data is None). Records stage as executed in ctx.stage_results.

tasks.label: AnomalyType registration
tasks.asserts: new enum members under '# --- Transformation ---' section: TRANSFORM_DRAWER_DROPPED, TRANSFORM_METADATA_COERCED, TRANSFORM_DUPLICATE_ID_DROPPED, TRANSFORM_INPUT_MISSING. Closed-registry contract preserved.

tasks.label: Report section
tasks.asserts: report['transformation'] returns {drawer_count, sample_ids, metadata_keys, wing_room_counts, length_profile, dropped_count} when ctx.transformed_data is non-None; remains None otherwise. text_renderer adds a Transformation section.

tasks.label: Purity contract test
tasks.asserts: AST walk over src/mempalace_migrator/transformation/ asserts no `import chromadb`, no `open(`, no `Path.write_*`, no subprocess. Same structural ban as M8.1 logging discipline, scoped to the writer ban.

phases.name: Reconstruction Stage
phases.description: Implement step_reconstruct: the only writer in the pipeline. Builds a fresh ChromaDB 1.5.7 palace at ctx.target_path from ctx.transformed_data via the public chromadb client API. Strictly atomic: any failure cleans up the partial target_path. Source material: import_drawers + ensure_target_is_safe in mempalace-mcp-bridge:scripts/palace_reconstruction_prototype.py.
non_goals: No bundle serialisation (export_drawers / load_export_bundle from the prototype are out of scope — the migrator runs in-process)
No retrieval / usage / MCP-runtime validation in this milestone (deferred per ROADMAP non_goals)
No new exit codes (5 is already reserved for reconstruct failure by M6.4)
No --force / --overwrite flag (refusal to overwrite is a hard contract, not a flag)
No write to source palace (source is opened read-only by extraction; reconstruction never touches the source path)

tasks.label: chromadb dependency
tasks.asserts: dependencies += ['chromadb>=1.5.7,<2']. Version pin matches the supported pair documented in README. Lock-file regenerated.

tasks.label: Target safety check (port ensure_target_is_safe)
tasks.asserts: ensure_target_is_safe(target_path) raises ReconstructionError if: path exists and is a file; path exists and is a non-empty directory. Path absent or empty directory is OK. No mkdir performed (caller's responsibility). Verified by tests/test_reconstruction_safety.py (9 tests).

tasks.label: ReconstructionResult dataclass
tasks.asserts: frozen dataclass: target_path (Path), collection_name (str), imported_count (int), batch_size (int), chromadb_version (str), target_manifest_path (Path).

tasks.label: Atomic batch insert (port import_drawers core)
tasks.asserts: reconstruct(ctx) -> ReconstructionResult. _writer.open_client uses chromadb.PersistentClient with anonymized_telemetry=False, allow_reset=False. Creates collection from TransformedBundle.collection_name + collection_metadata. Inserts via collection.add() in batches of BATCH_SIZE=500. On any exception after target_path.mkdir, _rollback_target() shutil.rmtree the directory (when did_create) or removes children (when pre-existing empty), and raises ReconstructionError. _writer is the only module with a module-level chromadb import (asserted by tests/test_reconstruction_purity.py).

tasks.label: Target manifest writer
tasks.asserts: writes TARGET_MANIFEST_FILENAME='reconstruction-target-manifest.json' at target_path with: format_version (int=1), created_at (UTC ISO Z), source_palace_path, detected_format, drawer_count, chromadb_version, mempalace_migrator_version, warnings list. Verified by tests/test_reconstruction_manifest.py (13 tests).

tasks.label: Pipeline wiring
tasks.asserts: step_reconstruct calls reconstruction.reconstruct(ctx) when ctx.target_path is set, else sets ctx.stage_skip_reasons['reconstruct']='no_target_path'. Removes the NOT_IMPLEMENTED anomaly emission. step_reconstruct on failure raises ReconstructionError -> exit 5. MIGRATE_PIPELINE = (detect, extract, transform, reconstruct, validate); PIPELINES['migrate'] registered.

tasks.label: AnomalyType registration
tasks.asserts: 8 new enum members: RECONSTRUCTION_INPUT_MISSING, TARGET_PATH_NOT_DIRECTORY, TARGET_PATH_NOT_EMPTY, CHROMADB_CLIENT_FAILED, CHROMADB_COLLECTION_CREATE_FAILED, CHROMADB_BATCH_INSERT_FAILED, TARGET_MANIFEST_WRITE_FAILED, RECONSTRUCTION_ROLLBACK. Plus stage_skip_reasons: dict[str, str] field on MigrationContext.

tasks.label: CLI: migrate subcommand
tasks.asserts: new `migrate` subcommand: `migrate SOURCE --target TARGET`. --target required (click.Path(file_okay=False, dir_okay=True) -- accepts existing dir so safety check returns exit 5 on non-empty target). PIPELINES['migrate'] registered. analyze and inspect continue to refuse --target (no --target option declared). Verified by tests/test_cli_migrate.py (7 tests).

tasks.label: Report section
tasks.asserts: REPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION bumped 4→5. report['reconstruction'] returns {target_path, collection_name, imported_count, batch_size, chromadb_version, target_manifest_path} when ctx.reconstruction_result is non-None, None otherwise. _stages_section consults ctx.stage_skip_reasons before stub-anomaly check. text_renderer adds 'reconstruction:' block (omitted when None or absent).

tasks.label: Source-mtime invariant test
tasks.asserts: after a successful migrate, sha256(source_palace/chroma.sqlite3) is byte-identical to the pre-run hash. Hard contract: reconstruction never touches source.

tasks.label: Atomicity test (mid-batch failure injection)
tasks.asserts: patches _writer.add_in_batches to raise on batch index 1 with BATCH_SIZE=2; asserts ReconstructionError(code='chromadb_batch_insert_failed'), target_path no longer exists on disk (rollback complete), ctx.reconstruction_result is None, RECONSTRUCTION_ROLLBACK + CHROMADB_BATCH_INSERT_FAILED/CRITICAL anomalies emitted. Also covers chromadb_client_failed and chromadb_collection_create_failed rollback paths (7 tests total).

phases.implementation_status: done — 743 tests passing (352 core + 332 adversarial + 59 hardening). Production modules: reconstruction/_safety.py, _types.py, _manifest.py, _writer.py (sole module-level chromadb import — AST-asserted), reconstructor.py. Pipeline wiring: step_reconstruct + MIGRATE_PIPELINE + PIPELINES['migrate']. CLI: `migrate SOURCE --target TARGET` subcommand; analyze/inspect continue to refuse --target. Report: REPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION bumped 4→5; reconstruction section structured-or-None; text_renderer adds Reconstruction block. Hardening baselines regenerated. Implementation-detail: writer coerces empty-dict metadata {} → None before collection.add() because chromadb 1.5.7 rejects empty dicts; preserves drawer count and id set, no anomaly emitted.

phases.name: Target Parity Validation
phases.description: Once reconstruction lands, lift target_record_count_parity and target_id_set_parity out of checks_not_performed and add document/metadata/embedding parity checks. Validation never opens the target in write mode. Source material: validate_reconstruction in mempalace-mcp-bridge:scripts/palace_reconstruction_prototype.py.
phases.implementation_status: done — all 14 tasks complete, 775 tests green (added 32 new tests). New module: validation/parity.py (5 parity checks, lazy chromadb import inside _open_target_readonly for startup-performance safety). validation/__init__.py branches on ctx.reconstruction_result; _skipped_when_no_reconstruction() returns 5 entries with reason='reconstruction_not_run'. 7 new AnomalyType members under '# --- Validation: target parity ---'. CheckFamily gains 'parity'; SkippedReason gains 'reconstruction_not_run'. EXPLICITLY_NOT_CHECKED shrunk 9→7 (target_record_count_parity + target_id_set_parity removed). Pre-existing IndentationError duplicates in pipeline.py and cli/main.py fixed.
non_goals: No retrieval-parity check (out of scope per ROADMAP non_goals)
No usage-scenario-parity check (out of scope)
No MCP-runtime check (out of scope)
No new severities, no new exit codes
No embedding-vector numeric equivalence (chromadb 1.x re-derives embeddings — only presence is meaningfully checkable)
No new top-level report keys, no REPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION bump (M11 only mutates contents of existing keys)
No baseline regeneration (M12 owns baselines/runtime_envelope.json and baselines/report_signatures.json)
No --force / --skip-validation / --strict-parity flag

tasks.label: AnomalyType registration (do this first — closed registry)
tasks.asserts: new enum members under '# --- Validation: target parity ---': TARGET_OPEN_FAILED, TARGET_RECORD_COUNT_MISMATCH, TARGET_ID_SET_MISMATCH, TARGET_DOCUMENT_HASH_MISMATCH, TARGET_METADATA_MISMATCH, TARGET_EMBEDDING_MISSING, TARGET_EMBEDDING_PROBE_INCONCLUSIVE. Closed-registry contract preserved (free-form anomaly types still rejected by _coerce_type).

tasks.label: Extend CheckFamily and SkippedReason literals
tasks.asserts: CheckFamily Literal gains 'parity'. SkippedReason Literal gains 'reconstruction_not_run' (and keeps 'stage_not_implemented' for backward-compat — no longer emitted).

tasks.label: validation/parity.py — read-only target reader
tasks.asserts: Module-level chromadb import is allowed here (and only here, alongside reconstruction/_writer.py — AST-asserted). _open_target_readonly(target_path, collection_name) returns (client, collection) with Settings(anonymized_telemetry=False, allow_reset=False). _iter_target_records(collection) pages via collection.get(limit=500, offset=N, include=['documents', 'metadatas']). On open failure: single TARGET_OPEN_FAILED/HIGH anomaly, every parity check returns inconclusive.

tasks.label: target_record_count_parity (lifted)
tasks.asserts: compares collection.count() to ctx.transformed_data.summary.drawer_count. status='passed' on equality. status='failed' + TARGET_RECORD_COUNT_MISMATCH/HIGH on inequality, evidence data {expected, actual}. status='inconclusive' on target open failure.

tasks.label: target_id_set_parity (lifted)
tasks.asserts: Builds set(transformed_ids) and set(target_ids) via paged scan. status='passed' iff both diffs empty. status='failed' + TARGET_ID_SET_MISMATCH/HIGH; evidence data {missing_in_target_count, unexpected_in_target_count, missing_sample (≤20 sorted), unexpected_sample (≤20 sorted)}. Sample order is deterministic (sorted).

tasks.label: target_document_hash_parity (new)
tasks.asserts: sha256 over UTF-8 bytes of drawer.document on both sides; compares on the intersection of id sets only (missing ids already reported by 14.5). status='failed' + TARGET_DOCUMENT_HASH_MISMATCH/HIGH; evidence data {mismatch_count, mismatch_sample (≤20 sorted ids)}. status='inconclusive' on open failure or empty intersection.

tasks.label: target_metadata_parity (new)
tasks.asserts: Both sides normalised via meta_for_compare(m) = m or {} so empty-dict transformed metadata compares equal to None returned by chromadb (M10 caveat: writer coerces {} → None). Pure dict equality otherwise. status='failed' + TARGET_METADATA_MISMATCH/HIGH; evidence data {mismatch_count, mismatch_sample (≤20)} where each sample is {id, missing_keys_in_target, extra_keys_in_target, value_diff_keys}. Values themselves NOT echoed (PII / size). status='inconclusive' on open failure or empty intersection.

tasks.label: target_embedding_presence (new, best-effort)
tasks.asserts: Probe call collection.get(limit=1, include=['embeddings']). On exception → status='inconclusive' + TARGET_EMBEDDING_PROBE_INCONCLUSIVE/MEDIUM, no further pages. On success → paged scan; ids with None or empty embedding list are recorded. status='failed' + TARGET_EMBEDDING_MISSING/MEDIUM (severity capped at MEDIUM by design — embeddings are re-derivable). Evidence data {missing_count, missing_sample (≤20)}.

tasks.label: Wire validate() to call run_parity_checks
tasks.asserts: Replace _SKIPPED_RECONSTRUCTION constant with a callable _skipped_when_no_reconstruction() returning all 5 parity ids with reason='reconstruction_not_run'. validate() branches: if ctx.reconstruction_result is None → checks_not_performed = those 5 entries; else outcomes.extend(run_parity_checks(ctx)) and checks_not_performed = (). validate() still never raises.

tasks.label: Shrink EXPLICITLY_NOT_CHECKED
tasks.asserts: EXPLICITLY_NOT_CHECKED drops 'target_record_count_parity' and 'target_id_set_parity'; new length is 7. The three new parity check ids are NOT added to this list (they are real checks, not disclaimers).

tasks.label: Tests: parity checks happy/sad paths
tasks.asserts: Per check: pass case (built target equals bundle), fail case (mutated target via separately-built ad-hoc dir), inconclusive case (open failure → 5×inconclusive + 1 TARGET_OPEN_FAILED). Embedding-probe inconclusive path tested via monkeypatched collection.get raising on include=['embeddings']. Empty-metadata coercion case (drawer with metadata={} → target stores None → comparator says passed).

tasks.label: Tests: AST allowlist for chromadb imports + forbidden methods
tasks.asserts: Extend (do not fork). Module-level chromadb import allowlist becomes {reconstruction/_writer.py, validation/parity.py}. Walk parity.py and forbid attribute-access call names: add, update, upsert, delete, modify, reset, create_collection, delete_collection, peek. Forbid the literal Settings(allow_reset=True) anywhere in parity.py.

tasks.label: Tests: target mtime invariant
tasks.asserts: Extend the existing source-byte-identity test to re-hash source/chroma.sqlite3 AFTER step_validate (currently after step_reconstruct only). Add test_validate_target_mtime_invariant: snapshot st_mtime_ns of target/chroma.sqlite3 before step_validate, assert equality after. Platform-skip is allowed only with explicit reason in the skip message.

tasks.label: Tests: validation skip-list shape and report integration
tasks.asserts: test_validation: when ctx.reconstruction_result is None, checks_not_performed has exactly 5 entries with reason='reconstruction_not_run'. test_report_builder: assert len(EXPLICITLY_NOT_CHECKED)==7 and the two parity literals are absent (and the three new parity ids are NOT added to the list either). test_cli_migrate: a successful migrate yields 5 parity CheckOutcomes with family='parity', all status='passed', confidence_summary.overall_band='HIGH'.

phases.name: Write-Path Adversarial + Hardening
phases.description: Extend the M7 adversarial corpus and the M8 baseline envelope to cover the new transform/reconstruct/parity stages. Same discipline as M7/M8: no new pipeline stages, no new exit codes, no new CLI flags, no new severities. This phase owns write-path hardening proofs such as rollback re-assertion, larger-batch stress, duplicate-id adversarial coverage, and post-write read-back verification. Defects discovered are fixed in the owning production module.
phases.implementation_status: done — all 11 tasks complete, 840 tests green (392 adversarial + 61 hardening + remaining core). 6 new adversarial test files. Baselines extended with migrate-success entry. Production fixes: TARGET_MKDIR_FAILED AnomalyType added (mkdir PermissionError was misclassified as TARGET_PATH_NOT_DIRECTORY); write_manifest centralised mkdir; rebaseline.py fixed (unique per-run targets, source in subdir, single main guard).
non_goals: No new pipeline stage, subcommand, or CLI flag
No new exit code (pinned set {0..10} frozen)
No new severity (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL closed)
No speculative AnomalyType additions (M10/M11 already registered the write-path anomalies)
No production print/logging/sys.std*.write (M8 AST sweep already covers the full tree)
No weakening of any M7 invariant or M8 tolerance
No opportunistic re-baselining of pre-existing non-migrate entries in the same PR as the migrate-success baseline
No end-to-end usability claim (owned by M13 / phase 16)
No retrieval/usage/MCP-runtime parity (explicit ROADMAP non_goals)

tasks.label: Adversarial: target safety
tasks.asserts: fixtures: target exists as file, target is non-empty directory, target on read-only filesystem (skipif for non-POSIX); each must produce exit 5, ReconstructionError, structured anomaly, no traceback w/o --debug.
tasks.note: 3 tests. target_path_not_directory for file target, target_path_not_empty for non-empty dir, target_mkdir_failed (not target_path_not_directory) for read-only parent.

tasks.label: Adversarial: mid-batch chromadb failure
tasks.asserts: monkeypatch chromadb collection.add to raise on batch N; assert exit 5, target_path absent post-run (rollback succeeded), source byte-identical, anomaly RECONSTRUCTION_ROLLBACK present.
tasks.note: 6 tests via in-process run_pipeline (not subprocess) so monkeypatching works. Covers first/last/mid batch failures.

tasks.label: Adversarial: transformation rejects-all
tasks.asserts: input where every drawer has blank id or non-string document; assert TransformedBundle.drawers is empty, transformation emits N drop anomalies, downstream reconstruction either skips or refuses with structured anomaly (no silent empty target).
tasks.note: 7 tests covering all-blank-ids and all-null-documents fixtures.

tasks.label: M7 invariants on extended corpus
tasks.asserts: the existing nine M7 invariants iterate over the new fixtures from 15.1/15.2/15.3 unchanged. No new invariants added; no existing invariant relaxed.
tasks.note: CORPUS extended with migrate-path entries. Invariant parametrisation unchanged.

tasks.label: Baseline: migrate-success entry
tasks.asserts: new baseline entry for `migrate` on the minimal valid chroma_06 fixture; recorded via tests/hardening/rebaseline.py in a reviewed PR; tolerance unchanged (50% wall-clock, 25% RSS).
tasks.note: migrate_baseline_valid entry added. rebaseline.py fixed: per-run unique target dirs + source in subdir so targets are scoped within TemporaryDirectory.

tasks.label: Stability: report-signature on migrate
tasks.asserts: report-signature stability re-asserted on a successful migrate run. Non-deterministic fields (run_id, started_at, target_manifest_path absolute, imported_count if non-deterministic) excluded from signature via the existing redaction map.
tasks.note: extract_report_signature extended to include reconstruct_stable {imported_count, collection_name, batch_size} while redacting target_manifest_path and chromadb_version.

tasks.label: Logging discipline on new modules
tasks.asserts: existing AST walk now sweeps src/mempalace_migrator/transformation/ and src/mempalace_migrator/reconstruction/ for forbidden call sites (print/logging/sys.std*.write). No new exception list.
tasks.note: test_sweep_covers_transformation_and_reconstruction_subtrees guard added.

tasks.label: Atomic rollback guarantee re-assertion
tasks.asserts: Across injected write-path failures, rollback removes the entire partial target and no residual on-disk state remains after the command exits with reconstruction failure.
tasks.note: Merged into 15.2 test file. All rollback tests use in-process pipeline.

tasks.label: Batch-size stress and large-dataset write-path envelope
tasks.asserts: Reconstruction remains structurally correct under larger batch counts and larger fixture sizes; no partial writes survive failure, and migrate stays within the hardening envelope tracked by baselines.
tasks.note: 4 parametrised tests (BATCH_SIZE-1, BATCH_SIZE, BATCH_SIZE+1, 2*BATCH_SIZE+1). In-process run_pipeline to avoid subprocess 30s timeout.

tasks.label: Duplicate-ID adversarial ingestion failure
tasks.asserts: If duplicate ids reach the write path, reconstruction fails explicitly, surfaces a structured anomaly, exits with reconstruction failure, and leaves no partial target behind.
tasks.note: Extraction drops all occurrences of a duplicated id (duplicate_embedding_ids anomaly). _FIXTURE_UNIQUE_IDS=1 (only 'ok' survives). Dedup anomaly assertion accepts both duplicate_embedding_ids and transform_duplicate_id_dropped.

tasks.label: Insert-count and read-back verification under adversarial conditions
tasks.asserts: After successful reconstruction, immediate read-back confirms the target is reopenable and inserted record count matches the transformed bundle; adversarial divergence is surfaced as an explicit failure rather than silent success.
tasks.note: 3 tests: count parity, id-set parity, document-hash parity read-back.

phases.name: End-to-End Migration Usability Gate
phases.description: Final product gate: prove the migrator is actually usable end-to-end on the supported version pair, not merely implemented stage-by-stage. This phase adds no new pipeline stage and no new exit code. It only verifies that the existing migrate path is operational, honest, and repeatable under the documented constraints.
non_goals: No new runtime features
No retrieval-parity / usage-parity / MCP-runtime validation
No broadening of supported formats
No weakening of existing honesty contracts
phases.implementation_status: done — tests/test_migrate_e2e.py implemented (7 tests, 16.1–16.7 all green). No new production code required. Reuses M12 migrate fixture helpers, M8 extract_report_signature, M7 check_no_forbidden_vocabulary, M11 parity check ids. 844 tests green (3 pre-existing timing-flaky performance baseline failures unrelated to M13).

tasks.label: Successful migrate command contract
tasks.asserts: Running `migrate SOURCE --target TARGET` on the minimal valid chroma_0_6 fixture exits 0, creates a readable ChromaDB 1.5.7 target, writes a target manifest, and produces a report whose stages show detect/extract/transform/reconstruct/validate as executed.

tasks.label: No skipped parity checks after successful migrate
tasks.asserts: On a successful migrate run, report.validation.checks_not_performed does not contain target_record_count_parity, target_id_set_parity, target_document_hash_parity, or target_metadata_parity.

tasks.label: Confidence and honesty gate
tasks.asserts: On a successful migrate run, validation.confidence_band is at least MEDIUM, no forbidden correctness vocabulary appears in the JSON report, and no stage reports success while carrying a hidden CRITICAL anomaly.

tasks.label: Repeatability on fresh targets
tasks.asserts: Two consecutive migrate runs against two different fresh target directories produce equivalent parity outcomes, equivalent imported_count, and stable report signatures modulo run_id/started_at/path-like fields.

tasks.label: End-to-end source invariance
tasks.asserts: Across the whole migrate command, the source palace remains byte-identical and mtime-identical before and after the run; this re-asserts the reconstruction and validation read-only contract at the full-command level.

tasks.label: Readable target smoke test
tasks.asserts: The reconstructed target can be reopened via chromadb.PersistentClient in a fresh process, the collection exists, and collection.count() matches report.reconstruction.imported_count.

tasks.label: Current-position promotion rule
tasks.asserts: current_position is allowed to move beyond M12 only when 16.1 through 16.6 are all done; until then, the project must not claim end-to-end usability.

current_focus.label: M13 End-to-End Migration Usability Gate — complete
current_focus.reason: All 7 M13 tasks (16.1–16.7) done. tests/test_migrate_e2e.py implemented and green. 16.7 self-consistency rule satisfied: ROADMAP.json current_position.milestone advanced to M13_done. 844 tests green total. M13 is the final planned milestone — no further milestone targets.
